justification: Both extractions are nearly identical in structure, content, and quality. They share the same skills lists, project classifications, and impact metrics. Minor wording differences (e.g., 'semantic re-ranking' vs 'intelligent re-ranking', 'patient-specific classifier training' vs 'model inference per patient') are negligible and neither is clearly superior. Both accurately capture all concrete metrics (1,000+ orders, 52% latency reduction, 98% time reduction, 83% accuracy, ~5 hrs/week saved), avoid fabricating skills, and maintain appropriate conservatism. Project classifications are implicitly correct (agentic AI, ML/analytics, full-stack) though neither output includes explicit taxonomy labels. The outputs are genuinely equivalent across all four dimensions.
